name: Cambridge & IB self-marking gap patterns (MarkScheme rubric analysis)
description: First-hand patterns when students self-mark Cambridge past papers and IB Diploma practice against a strict second pass aligned to official mark schemes and markbands. Updated periodically; not official exam board statistics.
creator: MarkScheme

variables.label: Method marks (M) under-awarded on first self-mark
variables.notes: Students award A marks from final answer but skip M marks when working is incomplete.

variables.label: Essay scripts placed one band high vs strict LoR read
variables.notes: Economics/humanities; band evidence not quoted from script.

variables.label: Net marks recovered after second scheme-aligned pass
variables.notes: Single-question retries; varies by subject. Largest gains on multi-step science and calculation papers.

variables.label: MCQ keys marked correct despite misread handwriting digit
AI-assisted first marks awarded above strict scheme read
variables.notes: Generic LLM marking rewards plausible wording without method marks or band evidence. Second human-or-scheme pass corrects most.

variables.label: Science structured Qs where precise term wording was missing
variables.notes: Biology and chemistry Paper 2/4; students paraphrase instead of using mark-scheme vocabulary.

variables.label: IB criterion responses placed one markband high on first self-read
variables.notes: Humanities and sciences; descriptor evidence not quoted. Preliminary IB cohort — refresh quarterly.

variables.label: Grade estimates using the wrong session or component set
variables.notes: Results-season spikes: students compare raw marks to last year's aggregate or a different paper variant — component codes must match.
